verses.text: Has God cast away His people? 'God forbid'; Paul himself is an Israelite. Not cast off. God has not abandoned Israel.
Has God cast away His people? 'God forbid'; Paul himself is an Israelite. Not cast off. God has not abandoned Israel.
As in Elijah's day, a remnant is reserved 'according to the election of grace,' not works. Grace, not works. The remnant stands by God's choosing.
As in Elijah's day, a remnant is reserved 'according to the election of grace,' not works. Grace, not works. The remnant stands by God's choosing.
As in Elijah's day, a remnant is reserved 'according to the election of grace,' not works. Grace, not works. The remnant stands by God's choosing.
As in Elijah's day, a remnant is reserved 'according to the election of grace,' not works. Grace, not works. The remnant stands by God's choosing.
Israel obtained not, but the election did; the rest were blinded. Two outcomes. Some are saved, the rest hardened.
Israel obtained not, but the election did; the rest were blinded. Two outcomes. Some are saved, the rest hardened.
Israel obtained not, but the election did; the rest were blinded. Two outcomes. Some are saved, the rest hardened.
Israel obtained not, but the election did; the rest were blinded. Two outcomes. Some are saved, the rest hardened.
Through their fall salvation came to the Gentiles, 'to provoke them to jealousy.' A purpose in the fall. Israel's stumble opened the door to the nations.
Through their fall salvation came to the Gentiles, 'to provoke them to jealousy.' A purpose in the fall. Israel's stumble opened the door to the nations.
Paul magnifies his office to save some; their casting away is 'the reconciling of the world.' Wider mercy. Even their loss serves the world's gain.
Paul magnifies his office to save some; their casting away is 'the reconciling of the world.' Wider mercy. Even their loss serves the world's gain.
Paul magnifies his office to save some; their casting away is 'the reconciling of the world.' Wider mercy. Even their loss serves the world's gain.
If the root be holy, so the branches; 'boast not against the branches.' No pride. The Gentile owes his standing to the holy root.
If the root be holy, so the branches; 'boast not against the branches.' No pride. The Gentile owes his standing to the holy root.
If the root be holy, so the branches; 'boast not against the branches.' No pride. The Gentile owes his standing to the holy root.
The branches were broken off for unbelief; 'be not highminded, but fear.' Stand in faith. Presumption invites the same fall.
The branches were broken off for unbelief; 'be not highminded, but fear.' Stand in faith. Presumption invites the same fall.
The branches were broken off for unbelief; 'be not highminded, but fear.' Stand in faith. Presumption invites the same fall.
Behold 'the goodness and severity of God'; if He spared not the natural branches, take heed. Solemn balance. Mercy and judgment both warn us.
Behold 'the goodness and severity of God'; if He spared not the natural branches, take heed. Solemn balance. Mercy and judgment both warn us.
Behold 'the goodness and severity of God'; if He spared not the natural branches, take heed. Solemn balance. Mercy and judgment both warn us.
Blindness in part till 'the fulness of the Gentiles be come in'; 'so all Israel shall be saved.' Future mercy. God's plan reaches a glorious end.
Blindness in part till 'the fulness of the Gentiles be come in'; 'so all Israel shall be saved.' Future mercy. God's plan reaches a glorious end.
Blindness in part till 'the fulness of the Gentiles be come in'; 'so all Israel shall be saved.' Future mercy. God's plan reaches a glorious end.
They are 'beloved for the fathers' sakes'; the gifts and calling of God are 'without repentance.' Unchanging call. God does not revoke His covenant love.
They are 'beloved for the fathers' sakes'; the gifts and calling of God are 'without repentance.' Unchanging call. God does not revoke His covenant love.
God 'concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.' Mercy the aim. He shuts up all to sin to show grace to all.
God 'concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.' Mercy the aim. He shuts up all to sin to show grace to all.
God 'concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.' Mercy the aim. He shuts up all to sin to show grace to all.
'O the depth of the riches... of God!'; 'of him, and through him, and to him, are all things.' Adoring wonder. Paul bows before the unsearchable wisdom of God.
'O the depth of the riches... of God!'; 'of him, and through him, and to him, are all things.' Adoring wonder. Paul bows before the unsearchable wisdom of God.
'O the depth of the riches... of God!'; 'of him, and through him, and to him, are all things.' Adoring wonder. Paul bows before the unsearchable wisdom of God.
